news.Title: Blue Origin faces months of delays after rocket explosion damages launch pad
news.Content: By Akash Sriram NEW YORK, May 30 (Reuters) - Blue Origin faces a months-long setback after the explosion of a rocket damaged its launch pad, company and industry sources said, scrambling schedules for Amazon satellite launches and bolstering SpaceX's dominance in the commercial launch market. The mishap, ‌which occurred during a test fire of the engines for the New Glenn rocket's launch next week, comes at a ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Blue Origin faces a significant setback of at least six months due to a rocket explosion damaging its launch pad.
• The incident occurred during a test fire of the New Glenn rocket, impacting schedules for Amazon's satellite launches.
• This delay jeopardizes Amazon's LEO constellation deployment timeline, which requires deploying half its satellites by July 2026 to meet regulatory deadlines.
• The explosion also affects Blue Origin's planned launch of its Blue Moon lunar lander and potential NASA lunar missions.
• The setback strengthens SpaceX's position in the commercial launch market, although SpaceX also experienced a similar launch pad incident in 2016.
news.Keywords: Launch vehicle, satellite deployment, regulatory deadline, commercial launch market, supply chain disruption

news.Title: MarketBeat Week in Review – 05/25 - 05/29
news.Content:
Key Points

Interested in Marvell Technology, Inc.? Here are five stocks we like better.

Stocks made a strong rally to close out the month after the consistent strength and breadth of tech earnings brought money off the sidelines.

Investors are cautiously optimistic about a potential extension to the ceasefire between the U.S. and Iran; for now, investors are looking to an eventual agreement.

Next week’s jobs report may set expectations for the direction of interest rates later this year.

Markets moved sharply higher to close the week and month, with technology stocks leading the way. The consistency of earnings reports is moving money off the sidelines as future revenue, and more importantly, earnings guidance, makes it clear that there’s plenty of room for the AI trade to run.

Investors were also casting an optimistic eye towards the Middle East. The U.S. and Iran have seemingly agreed to a 60-day extension to the existing ceasefire. But investors, for now, are looking through that ambiguity and are focused on the belief that a resolution will happen sooner rather than later.

→ AI’s Biggest Bottleneck Could Make These 2 Stocks Soar

The big economic news next week will be the jobs report on June 5. For now, the expectation is for the Federal Reserve to hold rates steady in June. But if hiring looks strong, then the odds of a rate hike in future meetings are likely to increase.

Articles by Thomas Hughes

→ Salesforce Stock Finds Support as AI Momentum Builds

If you’re wondering why the market is rallying this week, just focus on these articles from Thomas Hughes. The AI trade is playing out in hardware like the custom AI chips (ASICS) from Marvell Technology (NASDAQ: MRVL). The stock went parabolic ahead of its May 27 earnings report, and Hughes explained why a pullback is both likely and an opportunity.

This week also showed that concerns about the SaaS-pocalypse were unfounded. Snowflake (NYSE: SNOW) was a big winner, and Hughes noted that the snowballing effect of the company's AI flywheel will lead to further gains.

→ Apple’s Agentic AI Plans Could Be Its Biggest Growth Story Yet

Cybersecurity stocks are also coming back into fashion. However, ZScaler (NYSE: ZS) dropped this week after announcing strong AI spending. But Hughes pointed out why this could be a buy-the-dip opportunity.

Articles by Sam Quirke

As if the market wasn’t excited enough about the upcoming SpaceX (NASDAQ: SPCX) IPO, rumors are swirling that Elon Musk has plans to merge SpaceX and Tesla (NASDAQ: TSLA). This will be a developing story, but this week, Sam Quirke gave investors an overview of why the groundwork for that move is already in place.

Story Continues

Apple Inc. (NASDAQ: AAPL) has been one of the hottest stocks of 2026. Quirke explained how analysts believe the company’s proprietary ecosystem holds the key to the Agentic AI world as the conversation and dollars shift to the edge—where Apple has an entrenched advantage.

Quirke also covered the launch of Alexa for Shopping by Amazon.com Inc. (NASDAQ: AMZN). The product removes an unexpected source of friction for a company known for its seamless e-commerce experience. Shareholders appear to agree.

Articles by Chris Markoch

After being in a slide since October, Microsoft Corp. (NASDAQ: MSFT) is showing signs of life. Chris Markoch explained why analysts are taking note of the company’s AI catalysts. That could mean that MSFT won’t be on sale for much longer.

Many retail investors will stay away from the SpaceX IPO. However, Markoch highlighted three stocks under $50 that have moved higher because of their business with SpaceX, and why each could have much higher to move.

Retail earnings show that consumers are still spending. However, Markoch found details in some of the major retail earnings that suggest the consumer may not be as healthy as hoped, and how portfolios should be positioned as a result.

Articles by Ryan Hasson

Like many space stocks, Rocket Lab (NASDAQ: RKLB) got a bump after SpaceX released its S-1 filing. But this week, Ryan Hasson reminded investors that Rocket Lab is making news and winning business without the SpaceX halo, which is why analysts continue to reprice RKLB higher.

Hasson also summarized Alphabet’s (NASDAQ: GOOGL) Google I/O 2026 Developer Conference. The company unveiled its most ambitious suite of AI models and features. However, Hasson noted that, for now, investors are taking a wait-and-see approach on a stock that’s up 120% in the last 12 months.

Growth stocks, particularly technology stocks, continue to lift the market. But Hasson reminded investors with a 10-year time horizon why they shouldn’t forget about the compounding benefit of five high-yield dividend stocks and ETFs.

Articles by Leo Miller

The NVIDIA (NASDAQ: NVDA) earnings report has become the most important report for the broader market. But Leo Miller noted that, this earnings season, the upcoming report from Broadcom (NASDAQ: AVGO) may be 1A as investors are coming in with high expectations.

Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (NYSE: TSM) recently announced a 17% dividend increase. Miller explained what amount shareholders will receive and why it’s one of the more solid dividends in the tech sector.

If the market is about to broaden beyond tech stocks, Miller highlighted three stocks that have seen a combined $25 million of insider buying.

Articles by Nathan Reiff

Quantum computing stocks got a lift after an announcement that the federal government will provide incentives to a handful of domestic quantum companies. Nathan Reiff wrote about how the incentives will benefit two of the sector's current leaders.

Amidst all the tariff turmoil, it’s important to note that the Section 232 on aluminum products remains in place. Reiff explained why that’s bullish for two aluminum stocks that are poised for strong gains.

Articles by Dan Schmidt

When analysts raise their price targets on a company like Micron Technology (NASDAQ: MU), Dan Schmidt reminded investors that it’s important to look at complementary sector stocks that could get a lift. Schmidt highlighted five AI stocks that are rising in sympathy with MU and whether investors should view them as opportunities or hype.

Berkshire-Hathaway (NYSE: BRK.B) just released its first 13F in the Greg Abel era. Schmidt explained why the new CEO's moves show a commitment to value, albeit a nuanced shift towards a book that’s thinner but more highly concentrated.

Articles by Jeffrey Neal Johnson

Glasses aren’t just for improving eyesight. In fact, the inflection point for smart glasses has a new vision that’s focused on augmented reality. Jeffrey Neal Johnson summarized who the likely major players will be in this space, at least in the short term.

Johnson also highlighted the recent multi-million-unit ASIC order that Qualcomm (NASDAQ: QCOM) received from ByteDance. But that’s only one part of Qualcomm’s growth story, which could lead to a significant re-rating for QCOM.

Micron became the latest member of the $1 trillion market cap club. Johnson explained the company’s key role in the AI memory boom and why this may be just the beginning for MU.

Articles by Jennifer Ryan Woods

Retail stocks were in focus this week, and Jennifer Ryan Woods highlighted two specialty names that saw their stock prices rise after strong earnings reports. For Abercrombie & Fitch (NYSE: ANF), the stock climbed 12%, bolstered by the company reiterating its full-year guidance despite ongoing headwinds in the Middle East.

A similar story was in place for Ross Stores (NASDAQ: ROST). However, Ross Stores showed strong store traffic that led to a 17% increase in comparable store sales.

Articles by Peter Frank

When an investment bank delivers an earnings report highlighted by growing revenue and an increase to its dividend, the stock usually goes up. That’s why it’s noteworthy that shares of Piper Sandler (NYSE: PIPR) went down after its solid report. Peter Frank explained why investors focused on a limited bull case.

Frank also explained why AI tools and a platform expansion sent Rocket Companies (NYSE: RKT) back to profitability. However, the company’s near-term outlook will still depend on improvement in the housing market.

Insurance is about risk management. This could be a reason to look at Palomar Holdings (NASDAQ: PLMR). The company’s business model focuses on writing insurance policies that other companies won’t touch. It’s a high-risk model, but see why Frank noted that analysts believe PLMR is worth the risk.

The article " MarketBeat Week in Review – 05/25 - 05/29 " was originally published by MarketBeat.

View MarketBeat's top stocks for May 2026 .

news.Title: New Fed Chair Kevin Warsh May Face the Central Bank's Worst Nightmare: Stagflation
news.Content: It's been a history-making month in more ways than one for Wall Street. We've witnessed the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJINDICES: ^DJI) , S&P 500 (SNPINDEX: ^GSPC) , and Nasdaq Composite (NASDAQINDEX: ^IXIC) all romp to record-closing highs. We also observed a rare changing of the guard at America's foremost financial institution, the Federal Reserve. May 15 was the final day of Jerome Powell's second term as Fed chair. His final year was marked by harsh criticism from President Donald Trump over interest rates and the Federal Open Market Committee's (FOMC) lack of action in ...
news.AI_Summary:
• The article discusses the potential for stagflation in the U.S. economy under new Fed Chair Kevin Warsh.
• Stagflation is characterized by high unemployment, stagnant economic growth, and high inflation.
• Factors contributing to potential stagflation include global tariffs, the impact of the Iran conflict on oil prices, and a modest upward trend in unemployment.
• GDP growth has been inconsistent, averaging around 2% quarterly annualized since the start of 2025.
• The Federal Reserve faces a challenging scenario with no easy solutions to combat stagflation.
news.Keywords: Stagflation, monetary policy, inflation, unemployment, economic growth

news.Title: Is There a Future for the Cryptocurrency XRP?
news.Content: Down 43% over the last year, the cryptocurrency XRP (CRYPTO: XRP) continues to struggle even though the company behind the banking token, Ripple, keeps notching wins. ...
news.AI_Summary:
• The cryptocurrency XRP has declined 43% over the past year despite Ripple's legal and business wins.
• The core bull case for XRP, that Ripple's success drives XRP demand, is weakening.
• Banks primarily use Ripple's messaging and settlement layer, which does not interact with XRP.
• Ripple's cross-border payments system now offers an alternative stablecoin, RLUSD, which provides similar benefits without XRP's volatility.

news.Title: Trumpflation Isn't Close to Peaking -- and That's Terrible News for a Stock Market That's Priced for Perfection
news.Content: The stock market delivering outsize returns under President Donald Trump is nothing new. During his first, non-consecutive term, the ageless Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJINDICES: ^DJI) , benchmark S&P 500 (SNPINDEX: ^GSPC) , and growth stock-propelled Nasdaq Composite (NASDAQINDEX: ^IXIC) gained 57%, 70%, and 142%, respectively. Since Trump's second term began, the Dow, S&P 500, and Nasdaq Composite have surged another 16%, 25%, and 34%, respectively. On an annualized basis, Dow/S&P 500 returns under Trump have outpaced most other presidents since the late 1890s. Missed Nvidia in 2009? This Rare Signal Is Flashing ...
news.AI_Summary:
• The article argues that 'Trumpflation,' driven by tariffs and the Iran war impacting oil prices, is not peaking and poses a significant risk to a stock market priced for perfection.
• Inflation has surged from 2.4% to 3.8% year-over-year between February and April, with projections indicating a further rise to 4.18% in May, a three-year high.
• Tariffs on imported goods like steel increase domestic production costs, which are passed on to consumers, contributing to sticky price increases.
• The closure of the Strait of Hormuz due to the Iran war has significantly impacted fuel prices, acting as a primary driver of current inflation.
• The author suggests that the stock market's outperformance, fueled by AI and tax cuts, is unsustainable given the current inflationary pressures and geopolitical risks.
news.Keywords: Trumpflation, inflation, tariffs, oil prices, market valuation

news.Title: Stock Split Watch: Is Eli Lilly Next?

news.AI_Summary:
• Eli Lilly (LLY) is a strong growth stock, particularly in the obesity drug market, holding about 60% share in the U.S. GLP-1 drug market.
• The stock has advanced over 160% in the past three years and recently surpassed $1,000, prompting speculation about a potential stock split.
• Stock splits can lower the per-share price, making the stock accessible to a wider range of investors and signaling management optimism.
• Lilly has a history of stock splits, but the last one was in the late 1990s; a current split might depend on sustained trading above $1,000.
• The launch of its oral obesity drug, Foundayo, alongside injectables like Mounjaro and Zepbound, represents a significant new growth driver for the company.
news.Keywords: Stock split, obesity drugs, GLP-1 market, growth stock, revenue growth

• 輝達執行長黃仁勳罕見力挺英特爾，顯示AI晶片與高頻寬記憶體（HBM）趨勢下，台、美、韓產業形成競合關係。
• 英特爾與台積電在先進製程領域雖有競爭，但短期內英特爾仍高度依賴台積電的技術，雙方走向共生狀態。
• 韓國大廠如三星、SK海力士等積極與台灣半導體產業鏈串聯，以確保其記憶體產品能整合進AI伺服器架構。
news.Keywords: COMPUTEX,AI晶片,競合關係,半導體生態系,供應鏈整合

news.Title: 5-star analyst sets jaw-dropping SanDisk stock price target
news.Content: Barclays moved on SanDisk on May 27. The day after, Mizuho followed. When top-ranked analysts at two different firms raise targets on the same stock on back-to-back days, each arriving via a different methodology, the second note is worth reading carefully. Mizuho's argument is built around numbers that extend well beyond the last earnings report and further into the future than most coverage of this stock has focused on. What Mizuho changed and the analyst behind the call Vijay Rakesh, a five-star analyst at Mizuho Securities, raised his price ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Mizuho Securities analyst Vijay Rakesh raised SanDisk's (SNDK) price target to $1,825, implying 12% upside.
• Rakesh increased FY2027 revenue estimates to $45.3 billion (vs. $43.6B consensus) and EPS estimates to $184.95 (vs. $180.14 consensus).
• The bull case is founded on persistent NAND supply tightness expected through 2027, with non-AI customers undersupplied by 30-50%.
• Agentic AI is identified as a new growth driver, potentially increasing NAND demand from 2027 due to its requirement for more persistent storage.
• Near-term tailwinds include rising demand tied to High Bandwidth Flash and CMX rack deployments.
news.Keywords: NAND supply, agentic AI, revenue estimates, EPS estimates, structural tightness

news.Title: 沒有鴻海、廣達、緯創？投顧看好AI缺貨漲價潮 大推台積電、聯電、國巨、華邦電、技嘉等15檔一次評
news.Content: 台股5月狂漲5806點，只差不到50點就衝破45K了，全歸功AI掀起的缺貨漲價熱潮，法人看好8大產業共15檔個股，對目標價EPS全說了，大推台積電目標價2500元、聯電165元、聯發科5320元、國巨800元、華邦電1 ...

news.Content: 原本是一場討論股利與營運成果的 股東會 ，卻一路從29日上午9點開到30日凌晨3點28分才結束，耗時18小時28分鐘，刷新台股及 中華電信 最長紀錄，背後並非財報或配息爭議，其實是中華電信與工會之間延燒已久的勞資角力全 ...
news.AI_Summary:

• 董事長多次提醒股東會應回歸公司治理與股東權益討論，而非勞資會議。

news.Title: 台股瘋漲國內消費沒跟上？學者揭台股財富效果失靈真相
news.Content: 台股多頭氣勢如虹，2026年還沒過半，台股已經接連飛躍3萬點、4萬點大關，不論是咖啡廳、菜市場，都可聽到民眾談論股市行情，投資台股儼然成為全民運動。然而，股市大漲未能等比例反映在國內消費，學 ...
news.AI_Summary:
• 台股屢創新高，但國內消費成長力道相對溫和，學者分析財富效果帶動實體經濟的效果有限。
• 原因一為股市賺到的資金續留股市尋找更好標的，或用於房屋增貸、小額貸款等槓桿操作。
• 原因二為部分資金流向海外旅遊，導致國內服務業對景氣熱絡感受有限。
• 儘管主計總處上修民間消費實質成長率至3.6%，但國人在國外消費成長率更高。
• 學者擔憂，若股市漲勢無法轉化為實體經濟動能，且年輕人過度投入股市可能降低就業意願。
news.Keywords: 財富效果,國內消費,股市資金流向,海外旅遊,實體經濟

news.Title: Are Summer Headwinds Already Pricing Into Stocks?
news.Content: The average price-to-earnings ratio for the S&P 500 index (SNPINDEX: ^GSPC) over time is around 19x. Today, the S&P 500's P/E ratio is around 27.5x. That suggests that the market is not fully pricing in potential summer headwinds. And yet, markets often climb higher amid uncertainty. Here's what you need to know as you make investment decisions at the start of June. There is a material "wall of worry" An old Wall ...
news.AI_Summary:
• The S&P 500's P/E ratio of 27.5x is significantly higher than its historical average of 19x, suggesting potential overvaluation.
• Geopolitical conflicts in the Middle East are driving up energy prices, contributing to inflation and concerns about a global recession.
• Chevron CEO Mike Wirth indicates oil prices are more likely to rise due to reduced supply and impact on global oil reserves.
• Walmart provided a muted outlook for the rest of fiscal 2027 despite a strong first quarter, reflecting broader economic concerns.
• Conservative investors may consider increasing cash balances or focusing on value stocks like Coca-Cola, which offers a dividend yield above the market average.
news.Keywords: market headwinds, inflation, geopolitical risk, recession concerns, conservative investing

news.AI_Summary:

• 部分資金選擇留在股市尋求更高報酬，另一部分則流向海外旅遊，對實體經濟的刺激有限。

news.Title: Meta, TikTok, Snap, and YouTube settle school lawsuit for $27M
news.Content: Investing.com -- Meta Platforms, TikTok parent ByteDance, Snap, and Alphabet’s YouTube have agreed to pay roughly $27 million to settle claims brought by a Kentucky school district alleging their platforms contributed to a student mental health crisis, Reuters reported on Friday. ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Meta Platforms, TikTok, Snap, and YouTube have agreed to a $27 million settlement in a lawsuit filed by a Kentucky school district.
• Meta will pay the largest portion of the settlement at $9 million, with TikTok and Snap paying $8 million each, and YouTube paying $2.01 million.
• The lawsuit alleged that the platforms contributed to a student mental health crisis by designing features to keep young users engaged.
• The settlements do not require any admission of wrongdoing or changes to platform features.
• This case was seen as a bellwether for over 1,200 similar lawsuits filed by school districts across the US.
news.Keywords: litigation, settlement, mental health, social media, youth engagement

news.Title: Samsung AI bonus payouts spark debate over sharing tech boom gains - Bloomberg
news.Content: Investing.com -- Massive bonus payments tied to the artificial intelligence-driven chip boom are fueling debate in South Korea over how the benefits of the technology surge should be distributed, Bloomberg reported on Friday. The discussion has intensified after Samsung Electronics awarded ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Massive bonus payouts to Samsung Electronics employees in its memory chip division, some up to $400,000, are sparking debate in South Korea over wealth distribution from the AI boom.
• These bonuses were tied to strong earnings from AI-related demand and helped avert a potential strike.
• The disparity in bonuses, with employees outside the semiconductor division receiving significantly less, highlights concerns about widening income gaps.
• South Korea's chip exports surged 202% in early May, driven by memory chip makers like Samsung and SK Hynix.
• Policymakers are considering measures like a 'citizen dividend' or a sovereign wealth fund to share AI-driven gains.
news.Keywords: AI boom, semiconductor, bonus payouts, wealth distribution, economic inequality

news.Title: This Fund Manager Has a Brilliant Strategy for Investing in AI Stocks
news.Content: Gavin Baker, the Chief Investment Officer of the hedge fund Atreides Management, has established itself as one of the top tech investors operating today. In eight years managing the OTC Portfolio at Fidelity, Baker achieved a compound annual return rate of more than 19% and outperformed 99% of his peers on Morningstar. Will AI create the world's first trillionaire? Our team ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Gavin Baker, CIO of Atreides Management, believes the AI sector is 'cross-sectionally inefficient' regarding valuations.
• He notes that memory stocks (e.g., Micron) and Nvidia are trading at low P/E ratios, while power, cooling, and optical stocks (e.g., Lumentum, Coherent) have much higher multiples.
• Baker suggests that if the high multiples for optical stocks are justified, then memory and Nvidia stocks should increase significantly.
• Conversely, if Nvidia and Micron's multiples are correct, the higher-multiple stocks may underperform.
• The analysis favors cheaper AI-related stocks like Micron and Nvidia over those relying on multiple expansion for growth.
news.Keywords: AI sector, valuation, P/E ratio, semiconductor, investment strategy

news.Title: GlobalFoundries Maps Path to 40% Margins as Silicon Photonics Demand Builds
news.Content: Quantum Computing's Commercial Breakout Has Arrived GlobalFoundries (NASDAQ:GFS) Chief Financial Officer Sam Franklin said the chipmaker sees a path to significantly higher profitability over the next several years, driven by a mix shift toward faster-growing end markets, technology services, manufacturing productivity and better utilization of its existing footprint. Speaking at a TD Cowen event hosted by analyst Krish Sankar, Franklin said GlobalFoundries is targeting an exit gross margin of about 30% in 2026, 40% by the end of 2028 and 45% over the longer term. He said the company’s margin plan is tied to investments already made, customer design-win momentum ...
news.AI_Summary:
• GlobalFoundries (GFS) aims to significantly increase profitability, targeting a 40% gross margin by the end of 2028 and 45% long-term.
• Margin improvements are expected from a mix shift towards faster-growing end markets (communications, data center, automotive, IoT), technology services, and manufacturing efficiency.
• The company is increasing capital spending to $1.3-$1.4 billion this year, reflecting strong demand visibility and investments in areas like silicon photonics.
• GlobalFoundries targets a $1 billion silicon photonics run rate by the end of 2028 and $2 billion long-term, driven by pluggable optical transceivers and co-packaged optics.
• Technology services revenue is expected to grow to 12%-14% of total revenue long-term.
news.Keywords: gross margin, silicon photonics, capital expenditure, technology services, semiconductor

news.Title: Social media companies pay $27 million to settle Kentucky school district’s lawsuit over social media harms, records show
news.Content: By Diana Novak Jones May 29 (Reuters) - A Kentucky school district secured approximately $27 million in settlements from social media companies over claims they fueled a student mental‑health crisis, with Meta Platforms paying the largest amount at $9 million, according to records seen by ‌Reuters on Friday that reveal the settlement's financial terms for the first time. More from Yahoo Scout Why are ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Meta Platforms, Alphabet (Google), Snap, and ByteDance have settled a lawsuit with a Kentucky school district for approximately $27 million, related to social media's impact on student mental health.
• Meta paid $9 million, Alphabet $2.01 million, Snap $8 million, and ByteDance $8 million.
• The school district accused the companies of designing addictive platforms that fueled anxiety, depression, and self-harm among students.
• This case was considered a bellwether for over 1,200 similar lawsuits filed by school districts nationwide.

news.Title: The 180-Day Lease Holding Up SpaceX's $2 Trillion IPO
news.Content: Buried on page 13 of SpaceX's S-1 prospectus is the line item that has done more to justify a reported $2 trillion valuation than any other single disclosure in the filing. Anthropic, the rival AI company that Elon Musk publicly called "evil" earlier this year, is paying SpaceX $1.25 billion every month for compute capacity inside the Colossus 1 and Colossus 2 data centers in Memphis. The filing describes the payments running through May 2029. Annualized, that amounts to $15 billion a year. Spread across the full term, the deal totals roughly $45 billion. More News from Barchart ARM Stock ...
news.AI_Summary:
• SpaceX's potential $2 trillion IPO valuation is significantly influenced by a disclosed $1.25 billion monthly lease payment from Anthropic for compute capacity.
• This deal, initially appearing as a multi-year contract totaling ~$45 billion, was clarified by Elon Musk as a 180-day lease with a 90-day cancellation notice, significantly reducing its long-term value.
• SpaceX's AI segment revenue of $3.2 billion in 2025 (with $818 million in Q1 2026) is heavily reliant on this short-term Anthropic agreement, masking substantial losses in the segment.
• The clarification shifts the narrative from secured long-term revenue to a short-term rental, impacting investor perception of the AI infrastructure pivot and overall valuation.
news.Keywords: IPO valuation, compute capacity, lease agreement, AI infrastructure, revenue clarification

news.Title: MRVL Experiences Slow Expansion in Gross Margin: What's Ahead?
news.Content: Marvell Technology 's MRVL top line is growing on the back of custom AI silicon chips that are experiencing massive traction among hyperscalers. This traction comes from custom AI XPUs, electro-optics solutions and custom high bandwidth memory chips, which enable longer reach scale-up fabric connections ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Marvell Technology's revenue is growing due to custom AI silicon chips, but gross margin has shown inconsistent performance with a long-term declining trend.
• Non-GAAP gross margin declined 10 basis points sequentially to 58.9% in Q1 fiscal 2027, with projections for flat growth in Q2.
• Higher costs associated with manufacturing custom AI silicon may be impacting gross margin.
• Marvell faces significant competitive pressure in the custom silicon solutions space from Broadcom and Advanced Micro Devices.
• Despite margin challenges, Marvell's earnings estimates for fiscal 2027 and 2028 imply strong year-over-year growth.
news.Keywords: gross margin pressure, custom AI silicon, competitive landscape, revenue growth, semiconductor industry

news.Title: Meet the Newest Member of the Stock Market's Exclusive $1 Trillion Club (Hint: It's an AI Semiconductor Powerhouse)
news.Content: On Tuesday, May 26, Micron Technology (NASDAQ: MU) surpassed $1 trillion in market capitalization for the very first time, capping off an explosive 830% gain in its stock over the last 12 months. It's officially the third American semiconductor company to join the exclusive trillion-dollar club, behind Nvidia and Broadcom . Micron is one of the world's top suppliers of high-bandwidth memory (HBM) for the data center, a critical component in the artificial intelligence (AI) hardware stack. The company has incredible pricing power right now because demand for memory is heavily outstripping supply, which is fueling ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Micron Technology surpassed $1 trillion in market capitalization, achieving an 830% stock gain in the past 12 months.
• The company is a top supplier of High-Bandwidth Memory (HBM) for AI data centers, benefiting from strong demand and pricing power.
• Micron's HBM3E and upcoming HBM4 solutions offer significant performance and energy efficiency advantages, with Nvidia selecting them for upcoming GPUs.
• Fiscal 2026 second-quarter revenue surged 196% year-over-year to $23.8 billion, with cloud memory up 163% and mobile/client up 245%.
• Earnings per share exploded 756% to $12.07 in the second quarter, indicating rapid profit growth driven by the memory shortage.
news.Keywords: market cap, HBM, AI hardware, revenue growth, pricing power

news.AI_Summary:

news.Title: AMZN Deepens Its AI-Powered Shopping Push: Where Is the Stock Headed?
news.Content: Amazon AMZN is deepening its bid to dominate conversational commerce. On May 13, 2026, it folded its Rufus shopping assistant and the Alexa+ assistant into a single agentic tool, Alexa for Shopping, now free to U.S. customers across the Amazon app, website, and Echo Show with no Prime membership or device required. The system carries a shopper's history ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Amazon is integrating its Rufus and Alexa assistants into a single AI tool, 'Alexa for Shopping,' to enhance conversational commerce.
• The AI shopping assistant drove nearly $12 billion in incremental sales in 2025, with over 300 million customers using Rufus.
• AWS is offering its Agentic Shopping Assistant architecture to external retailers, with Tapestry's Kate Spade as an early adopter.
• Advertising revenues rose 24% to $17.2 billion in Q1, and net sales climbed 17% to $181.5 billion.
• Trailing-12-month free cash flow fell to $1.2 billion due to increased AI-related equipment purchases, and Q2 guidance implies margin pressure.

news.Title: Meta Platforms Could Open Up a Massive New Revenue Stream
news.Content: Meta Platforms (NASDAQ:META) is typically considered one of the four big hyperscalers in the tech sector. The other three major hyperscalers are Amazon ...
news.AI_Summary:

• The company plans over $100 billion in capital expenditures this year for data centers and AI infrastructure.
• Launching a cloud business would diversify Meta's revenue base, which is currently heavily reliant on advertising.
• Competitors like Amazon, Microsoft, and Alphabet are experiencing accelerating revenue growth and high margins in their cloud divisions.
• Meta's stock trades at a P/E ratio of 23, with 33% revenue growth reported in Q1, suggesting potential for stock appreciation with a cloud offering.

news.Title: Can KLA's Greenfield Fab Exposure Spark Multi-Year Revenue Growth?
news.Content: KLA Corporation KLAC appears well-positioned to capitalize on the semiconductor industry’s next wave of greenfield fab investments, driven largely by booming AI infrastructure demand and expanding memory capacity requirements. Its strong exposure to process control intensity at advanced nodes could become a major catalyst for multi-year revenue growth. In the fiscal ...
news.AI_Summary:
• KLA Corporation is well-positioned to benefit from greenfield fab investments driven by AI infrastructure demand and memory capacity requirements.
• The company reported fiscal Q3 2026 revenues of $3.42 billion, up 11% year-over-year, with Semiconductor Process Control revenues up 13%.
• Greenfield opportunities in DRAM and NAND are expected to increase into 2027, supported by AI-driven demand for HBM and advanced logic chips.
• Process control intensity is rising due to complex chip architectures, larger die sizes, and advanced packaging.
• KLA expects its advanced packaging business to generate over $1 billion in revenue this year and continues to generate robust free cash flow, returning capital to shareholders.
news.Keywords: semiconductor equipment, greenfield fabs, process control, AI demand, free cash flow

news.Title: TikTok Pushes Deeper Into Music as Label Relationships Shift
news.Content: This article first appeared on GuruFocus . TikTok's music strategy is becoming a sharper investor signal for ByteDance's broader platform ambitions, as the company appears to be pulling back from traditional label-facing relationships while moving ...
news.AI_Summary:
• TikTok is deepening its involvement in music, focusing on direct artist services, distribution, and advertising-related licensing, shifting away from traditional label relationships.
• Initiatives like SoundOn (music distribution) and a commercial music library for ads aim to give TikTok more control over its music ecosystem.
• TikTok has launched a partnership with Apple allowing Apple Music subscribers to listen to full tracks within TikTok.
• Despite industry tensions and layoffs in its music division, TikTok remains a crucial platform for the music industry due to its billion-plus monthly users.
• ByteDance, TikTok's parent company, views music more as a cost center but is exploring ways to reshape music economics on its own terms.
news.Keywords: music strategy, direct artist services, advertising licensing, platform ambitions, ecosystem control

news.Title: AMD Takes Quieter China Path
news.Content: This article first appeared on GuruFocus . Advanced Micro Devices ( AMD , Financials ) is taking a quieter approach in China than ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) is pursuing a less public strategy in China compared to Nvidia, but the market remains crucial.
• China accounts for approximately 20% of AMD's revenue across PCs, gaming, and some data center products.
• AMD holds about 4% of China's AI chip market and offers alternatives to Nvidia's CUDA through its ROCm open-source software platform.
• U.S. export controls limit the sale of advanced chips in China, affecting both Nvidia and AMD.
• AMD's software tools are less mature than Nvidia's, and export regulations pose challenges in the Chinese market.
news.Keywords: China market, AI chips, export controls, software platform, market share

news.Title: Tesla Robotaxi Fleet Trails Waymo in Texas
news.Content: This article first appeared on GuruFocus . Tesla Inc. ( TSLA , Financials ) is still in the early innings of ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Tesla's robotaxi fleet in Texas is significantly smaller than Waymo's, with only 42 authorized vehicles compared to Waymo's much larger fleet.
• This data highlights that scaling the robotaxi service remains a gradual process for Tesla, despite years of discussion about self-driving technology.
• Key factors for robotaxi deployment include regulatory clearance, safety performance, operational reliability, and public trust.
• Waymo currently holds a lead in Texas deployment due to its larger operational footprint.
• Tesla's next challenge is to translate its technology roadmap into a commercially viable and larger fleet.
news.Keywords: robotaxi, autonomous driving, fleet size, Texas market, commercial scaling

news.Title: Google India Ad Ruling Raises Trademark Risk
news.Content: This article first appeared on GuruFocus . Alphabet ( GOOGL , Financials ) is facing fresh scrutiny in India ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Alphabet (Google) has been ordered by the Delhi High Court to pay $31,600 in damages for trademark infringement in India.
• The court ruled that Google allowed competitors to use a company's brand name as an advertising keyword.
• This ruling could empower Indian businesses to challenge keyword advertising practices, potentially impacting Google's search advertising model.
• Search advertising relies heavily on keyword bidding, making Google vulnerable to increased legal risk in India if more such challenges arise.
• The case highlights potential changes to the economics of online advertising, where brands often pay to protect their names from rivals.
news.Keywords: trademark infringement, keyword advertising, legal risk, India market, search advertising

news.Title: Tesla Stock Fans Just Got Worrying News From Texas
news.Content: This article first appeared on GuruFocus . Tesla ( NASDAQ:TSLA ) has a much smaller robotaxi footprint in Texas ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Tesla has a substantially smaller robotaxi presence in Texas compared to Waymo, with only 42 authorized vehicles versus Waymo's 577.
• Other autonomous vehicle companies like Avride (Nebius), Nuro, and Zoox (Amazon) also have larger authorized fleets in Texas than Tesla.
• New Texas regulations have tightened oversight of commercial self-driving vehicles, with authorization rules becoming enforceable.
• Tesla launched its Texas robotaxi pilot in June 2025, while Waymo launched in March of the same year.
• Tesla's service is currently limited to parts of Austin, Dallas, and Houston, whereas Waymo operates in multiple major cities across Texas.
news.Keywords: robotaxi, autonomous vehicles, Texas regulations, fleet deployment, competitive landscape

news.Title: Can CRUS' Audio Converter Launch Diversify Revenue Streams & Boost Sales?
news.Content: The professional and prosumer audio industry is evolving rapidly. Against this backdrop, Cirrus Logic CRUS has recently introduced a new portfolio of nine audio converters, including analog-to-digital converters (ADCs), digital-to-analog converters (DACs) and CODECs, designed to deliver premium audio quality while maintaining an optimized price-to-performance ratio. The initiative aims to expand CRUS’ audio converter portfolio and reinforces its ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Cirrus Logic (CRUS) introduced nine new audio converters (ADCs, DACs, CODECs) aimed at the professional and prosumer audio market, estimated at $9 billion globally.

news.Title: Marvell Technology Reports Strong FCF and Outlook - Could MRVL Move Higher?
news.Content: Marvell Technology, Inc. ( MRVL ) , the semiconductor chip maker, generated strong Q1 free cash flow (FCF) and FCF margins, and dramatically hiked its FY 2028 revenue forecast due to strong AI-related demand. That could raise its FCF forecast, and MRVL stock could be worth 23% more, over $251 per share, as this article will show. MRVL closed up 3% on Thursday, May 27, to $204.83 after its May 27 release of Q1 FY 2027 results for the period to May 2. MRCL has been on a massive run-up in the last 2 months from March 30 ($87.81, +133%). ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Marvell Technology (MRVL) reported strong Q1 free cash flow (FCF) and margins, significantly raising its FY 2028 revenue forecast to $16.5 billion due to AI demand.
• The company's stock is up 133% in the last two months, accelerating after a partnership with Google for AI chips.
• FY 2028 revenue forecast is $1.5 billion higher than previously expected, indicating strong chip orders and locked-in profitability.
• Q1 operating cash flow was $638.8 million (26.4% of revenue), up 92% year-over-year, with TTM FCF at $1.665 billion (19.1% of revenue).
• Projected FCF of $3.3 billion (assuming a 20% margin) suggests a potential fair value of $220 billion, implying a 22.9% upside from current market cap.
news.Keywords: free cash flow, revenue forecast, AI demand, semiconductor, valuation

news.AI_Summary:

news.Title: SpaceX and This Nuclear Stock Could Turn $10,000 Into a Fortune
news.Content: SpaceX is expected to go public this summer, perhaps as early as June. While there are ways to buy into SpaceX today , most investors are better off simply buying shares during the initial public offering . While SpaceX's valuation is supposed to be huge -- experts believe the company is targeting a market cap between $1.5 trillion and $2 trillion -- one opportunity ...
news.AI_Summary:
• SpaceX is expected to go public with a potential market cap between $1.5 trillion and $2 trillion, and is exploring orbital data centers (ODCs) to address AI energy needs.
• The AI industry faces growth bottlenecks due to the energy intensity of data centers, with global spending projected to reach $7 trillion by 2030.
• Oklo, a nuclear energy company backed by Sam Altman, is developing small modular reactors (SMRs) to provide power for data centers.
• Both SpaceX's ODCs and Oklo's SMRs aim to solve the energy resource bottleneck for scaling AI infrastructure, though ODCs face significant technical challenges.
news.Keywords: IPO, data center infrastructure, energy resources, nuclear energy, SMRs, orbital data centers

news.Title: AMD Could Be the Biggest Winner in the Next AI Battle

news.AI_Summary:

• AMD's server CPU revenue is projected to rise to $44 billion by 2028 from $17 billion in 2026, with AI-driven earnings power estimated at $7 per share.

• Arm-based chips could capture 50%-75% of the future agentic AI CPU market, while Nvidia and Intel benefit from broader AI infrastructure demand.

news.Title: SpaceX Invests 3X More on AI Than Rockets and Lost $6.3 Billion on the Segment Last Year. Should Investors Be Worried?
news.Content: SpaceX's planned initial public offering (IPO) on June 12 is one of the most anticipated public debuts in years. Many savvy investors are trying to figure out where the company could be headed during the next few years, using information from the company's recent S-1 filing with the Securities and Exchange Commission. Tesla (NASDAQ: TSLA) chief executive officer and SpaceX founder Elon Musk has touted his company's focus on sending humans to Mars and its intentions to "transform the rocket launch industry into airline-like operations." Will AI create the world's first trillionaire? Our team just released a report on a ...
news.AI_Summary:
• SpaceX is investing significantly more in AI (over 3x) than in its rocket segment, leading to substantial operating losses of $6.3 billion in AI in the last year.
• The company's AI spending is focused on building large data centers (Colossus and Colossus II) to train advanced AI models like Grok 5.
• SpaceX plans to mitigate AI data center energy consumption and location issues by building orbital data centers powered by constant solar energy.
• Despite rising AI costs, SpaceX secured a $1.2 billion per month cloud services agreement with Anthropic, and estimates the AI TAM at $26.5 trillion.
news.Keywords: AI investment, capital expenditure, operating loss, data centers, orbital infrastructure

news.Title: Is Nvidia a Top Dividend Stock?
news.Content: Nvidia (NASDAQ: NVDA) has arguably been the quintessential growth stock over the past three years. The chipmaker has increased its revenue and earnings much faster than its similarly sized peers, while delivering extraordinary returns. That's why Nvidia's status as a high-growth company can hardly be denied, even by the bears. But what about the semiconductor specialist's dividend profile? news.Title: S&P Futures Gain on Hopes for U.S.-Iran Deal; Dell Pops on Blowout Earnings
news.Content: June S&P 500 E-Mini futures (ESM26) are trending up +0.18% this morning as investors became more confident that the U.S. and Iran are nearing a deal. The U.S. and Iran have reportedly reached a tentative deal to extend the ceasefire by 60 days, which would include the reciprocal reopening of the Strait of Hormuz during the first 30 days. It would mark the first phase of a multistage framework, which the U.S. hopes will result in Iran scaling back its nuclear program for decades. “We perhaps have the makings of a deal here,” Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said on Thursday. ...

• S&P futures gained on hopes of a U.S.-Iran deal, potentially leading to a rise in risk assets and a fall in rates.

• Arm Holdings (ARM) and Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) experienced notable stock price increases (+10% and +4% respectively).
• Economic data showed mixed signals, with moderating GDP growth and stable personal spending, while inflation remains a concern.

news.Title: VYM vs. SCHD: Which U.S. Dividend ETF Is a Better Buy?
news.Content: When people decide to buy dividend stocks, they're usually looking for safety. That doesn't mean these stocks are "safe," because no stocks are. All stocks carry a risk of loss. But investing in high-yield dividend stocks often means buying into high-quality companies with strong balance sheets. Dividend exchange-traded funds (ETFs) might not be flashy like tech stocks, but people don't buy dividend ETFs expecting rapid growth. Instead, investing in these funds is a matter of "slow and steady wins the race." This type ...
news.AI_Summary:
• The article compares two U.S. dividend ETFs, Vanguard High Dividend Yield ETF (VYM) and Schwab U.S. Dividend Equity ETF (SCHD), for investors seeking safety and steady income.
• Both ETFs have underperformed the S&P 500 over the past five years but are outperforming year-to-date in 2026.
• VYM holds over 600 stocks with a 2.24% dividend yield and has delivered a 29.5% return in the past year.
• SCHD holds 103 stocks with a 3.29% dividend yield and has outperformed the S&P 500 year-to-date.
• The ETFs offer diversification away from major tech names, though VYM includes Broadcom, a significant AI stock.
news.Keywords: dividend ETFs, income investing, portfolio diversification, market volatility, yield

news.Title: Tesla Stock Could Soar in the Second Half of 2026. Here's Why.
news.Content: Tesla (NASDAQ: TSLA) has not performed well this year. The stock is down 1% as of writing, while the S&P 500 has climbed 10%. Although Tesla's first-quarter results released on April 22 were pretty good on the surface -- revenue jumped 16% year over year to $22.4 billion, even amid the electric vehicle (EV) industry losing ground in the U.S. -- investors are concerned about Tesla's runaway spending. The company now projects capex spending for 2026 to be over $25 billion, above its previous guidance of $20 billion. There is skepticism regarding whether Tesla can justify these investments, especially given ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Tesla's stock has underperformed the S&P 500 year-to-date, despite a 16% revenue jump in Q1 to $22.4 billion.
• Investors are concerned about Tesla's increased capex spending projection of over $25 billion for 2026.
• The company's Optimus Gen 3 humanoid robot reveal, expected around late July/August, could be a significant catalyst if successful.
• Progress in Tesla's robotaxi service expansion and the development of unsupervised Full Self-Driving (FSD) software are also key potential drivers.
• Despite high valuation multiples (208x forward earnings), Tesla possesses significant long-term growth potential if execution is strong.
news.Keywords: capex spending, humanoid robots, robotaxi, autonomous driving, valuation multiples

news.Title: 2 Monster EV Stocks Chasing a $10 Trillion AI Opportunity
news.Content: Don't confuse electric vehicle (EV) stocks with traditional automotive stocks . In my view, most conventional auto stocks are akin to most manufacturing businesses. news.Title: 5 Warren Buffett Stocks to Hold Forever
news.Content: Warren Buffett is no longer the CEO of his investment vehicle, Berkshire Hathaway (NYSE: BRKA) (NYSE: BRKB) , but the celebrated investor is leaving a long shadow. news.Content: By Eduardo Baptista and Liam Mo BEIJING, May 29 (Reuters) - When AMD CEO Lisa Su arrived in China last week just days after Nvidia 's CEO left, she kept a much lower profile than Jensen Huang, who drew crowds in the centre of the capital as he posed for photos and ate local ‌delicacies like Beijing-style soybean paste noodles. Their contrasting approaches show two very different forms of corporate diplomacy around the world's ...

news.Title: At Computex, Nvidia and Taiwan's expanding role in AI infrastructure set to take centre stage
news.Content: By Wen-Yee Lee TAIPEI, May 29 (Reuters) - At Taiwan's annual Computex trade show next week, the spotlight is likely to be dominated, as usual, by Nvidia and its products, but also by the island's central and growing role in AI infrastructure. Nvidia CEO Jensen ‌Huang, arriving in Taipei more than a week ahead of the show, more than made that ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Nvidia and Taiwan's role in AI infrastructure are set to be central themes at the upcoming Computex trade show.
• Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang announced plans for the company to spend up to $150 billion annually in Taiwan, calling it the 'epicenter of the AI revolution'.
• AMD CEO Lisa Su also committed to investing over $10 billion in Taiwan's AI sector, co-investing with local partners for 2026 expansion.
• Taiwan's AI ecosystem is expanding from semiconductor manufacturing to broader infrastructure, including server manufacturers, packaging, and component suppliers.

news.Title: 71.6% of Nvidia's Portfolio Is Invested in These 2 Top Artificial Intelligence (AI) Stocks
news.Content: Nvidia (NASDAQ: NVDA) is at the center of the artificial intelligence (AI) revolution, and the company is trying to secure its future in the industry. One way it is doing so is through heavy R&D investments. Another is by investing in other promising AI-focused companies. Two of them, Intel (NASDAQ: INTC) and CoreWeave (NASDAQ: CRWV) , accounted for about 71.6% of its portfolio as of March 31. Given that Nvidia ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Nvidia (NVDA) has invested significantly in Intel (INTC) and CoreWeave (CRWV), representing 71.6% of its portfolio as of March 31.
• Intel is highlighted for its role in the server CPU market for agentic AI, with revenue up 7% YoY to $13.6 billion and adjusted EPS soaring 123% to $0.29 in Q1.
• Despite strong EPS growth, Intel's stock has risen nearly 500% in 12 months and trades at a high forward P/E of 151.5x, suggesting it may be overvalued.
• CoreWeave, an AI infrastructure provider, reported Q1 revenue of $2.1 billion, up 111.6% YoY, with a backlog of $99.4 billion.
• CoreWeave is not yet profitable, with operating expenses growing faster than revenue, leading to squeezed margins and net losses, despite significant revenue growth.
news.Keywords: AI investment, CPU market, agentic AI, infrastructure provider, valuation concerns

news.Title: Is NVIDIA (NVDA) the Most Profitable Stock to Invest In?
news.Content: NVIDIA Corporation (NASDAQ:NVDA) is one of the 12 Most Profitable Stocks to Invest In . On May 23, 2026, NVIDIA Corporation (NASDAQ:NVDA) CEO Jensen Huang asked Super Micro Computer (SMCI) to tighten its compliance after Taiwan detained three people for allegedly making fraudulent declarations tied ...
news.AI_Summary:
• NVIDIA reported Q1 adjusted EPS of $1.87 and revenue of $81.6B, exceeding consensus estimates.
• Raymond James raised its price target to $330 and maintained a Strong Buy rating, citing strong Q1 results, Q2 guidance, inference-led growth, and share gains.
• BofA also raised its price target to $350, maintained a Buy rating, and increased FY27/FY28 EPS estimates by 9% and 15% respectively.
• CEO Jensen Huang highlighted the accelerating buildout of AI factories and the scaling of agentic AI across industries, positioning Nvidia at the center of this transformation.
news.Keywords: Q1 earnings beat, price target raise, inference growth, AI factories, agentic AI

news.Title: Here’s Why Meta (META) is Among the 12 Most Profitable Stocks to Invest In
news.Content: Meta Platforms, Inc. (NASDAQ:META) is one of the 12 Most Profitable Stocks to Invest In . On May 24, 2026, Meta Platforms, Inc. (NASDAQ:META) Technology chief Andrew Bosworth was reportedly working to transform the ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Meta Platforms is transforming its workforce using AI, with agents expected to perform tasks and employees to review and improve their output.
• CEO Mark Zuckerberg indicated no further company-wide layoffs are expected this year, following a 10% workforce reduction and reassignment of 7,000 employees to AI teams.
• Wells Fargo lowered its price target on META to $765 from $770 but maintained an Overweight rating, citing improving market confidence in companies monetizing compute investments.
• Meta develops products for connection and sharing across various devices, including VR headsets and AI glasses.
news.Keywords: AI workforce transformation, AI teams, compute monetization, price target adjustment, Overweight rating

news.Title: Blackstone (BX) to Invest $5B in TPU Cloud Joint Venture with Google

news.AI_Summary:

news.Title: Best 3 Blue Chip Stocks to Buy After a Market Pullback -- Including Microsoft (MSFT) Stock
news.Content: It's tempting to want to fill your portfolio with lots of growth stocks. The best do offer chances at astronomical returns -- but they can also be overvalued and just as likely to pull back as to advance. So consider including some blue chip stocks in your mix. A blue chip stock is one tied to a relatively stable, large, established company. blue chip stocks often pay dividends, which can be a big plus. While they may not grow as briskly as some growth stocks will, they can be less volatile. Actually, some blue chip stocks offer impressive growth, too! ...
news.AI_Summary:

news.Title: Analysis-Huawei bets on speed over shrinking transistors to sidestep US chip sanctions
news.Content: By Eduardo Baptista and Che Pan BEIJING, May 29 (Reuters) - Huawei's new chip design principle focused on boosting transmission speed rather than continuing to shrink semiconductors offers a path for China to build cutting-edge chips despite U.S. sanctions, though whether it represents a true breakthrough remains to be seen. More from Yahoo Scout What challenges does Huawei's new approach face? How do U.S. sanctions impact China's chipmaking? What is Huawei's Tau Scaling Law approach? Is LogicFolding truly different from ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Huawei is developing a new chip design approach focused on transmission speed (Tau Scaling Law) to circumvent US sanctions, rather than shrinking transistors.
• This method, using techniques like LogicFolding, aims to improve chip density, efficiency, and clock speeds, potentially extending progress beyond Moore's Law.
• Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang commented that Huawei's approach is not a threat to TSMC, as TSMC has been using advanced 3D packaging for years.
• Analysts caution that stacking multiple chip layers increases power density and risks overheating, and that production yields and costs remain significant barriers.
news.Keywords: US sanctions, chip design, Tau Scaling Law, advanced packaging, TSMC

news.Content: 一棵參天大樹能長成今天的模樣，從來不是 一朝一夕之功 ，而是數十年前有人在烈日下掘土、埋種、澆灌的結果。今天台灣引以為傲的半導體產業與AI供應鏈榮景，正是那一輩人在最艱困的年代裡，為我們種下的「乘涼樹」。我們今天所享有的經濟 ...

• 作者質疑當代是否僅在收割前人成果，並未為後代種下新的成長樹木，尤其在AI時代的社會結構劇烈變動下。
• 文章指出，AI的指數級成長將導致比「數位鴻溝」更殘酷的「AI鴻溝」，掌握AI者將指數級累積財富與話語權。
• 作者批評部分政治人物過於關注短期選票與GDP數據，缺乏當年「不計任期、只問國運」的戰略決心，並擔憂政治人物利用AI同溫層效應進行社會分化。
news.Keywords: AI鴻溝,產業發展,戰略佈局,社會分化,世代傳承

news.Title: AI耗電激增　台積電：能源效率成晶片發展關鍵
news.Content: （中央社阿姆斯特丹2026年5月28日綜合外電報導）台積電高層主管今天指出，人工智慧（AI）帶動的用電需求激增，已使能源效率而非運算能力，成為未來電腦晶片發展的主要限制因素 ...
news.AI_Summary:
• 台積電高層主管指出，AI帶動的用電需求激增，已使能源效率成為未來晶片發展的關鍵限制因素，而非單純的運算能力。
• 客戶越來越重視「在不增加耗電下提升效能」，因為電力成本與供電可得性是營運商面臨的壓力。
• 台積電預計從2奈米製程到2028年的A14製程世代，晶片耗電量可降低最多30%，運算效能提升20%以上。
• 除了提高電晶體密度，先進封裝、晶片堆疊及光子技術等方案正變得愈來愈重要以提升效率。
• 文章也提及中國競爭對手華為的「韜（τ）定律」計畫，並指出其受限於無法取得EUV設備，凸顯能源效率對次世代AI晶片的重要性。
news.Keywords: AI,能源效率,晶片發展,製程技術,功耗

news.Title: 智寶AI動畫助手AIFRED導入聯發科技術，搶食全球動畫商機
news.Content: 【財訊快報／記者戴海茜報導】近年隨著動畫、遊戲屢創現象級熱潮，智寶國際(7824)AI動畫助手AIFRED導 ...
news.AI_Summary:
• 智寶國際（7824）將其AI動畫助手AIFRED導入聯發科技（2454）的LoRA Fusion技術，以搶攻全球動畫市場商機。
• 此技術能直接在PC或行動裝置上執行文生圖，快速生成幾乎無損的圖像，預計2030年全球動畫市場規模將達600.7億美元。
• AIFRED結合了聯發科技的裝置端生成式AI技術、中雍科技AI MEMC動態補償技術，以及JP GAMES的PWK開發套件，旨在提高動畫師生產力並快速生成3D角色。
• 智寶國際強調，AIFRED整合聯發科技GenAI Security加密技術，可在認證裝置上生成具特定特徵的角色，確保IP安全並防止未經授權的複製與分發。
news.Keywords: AI動畫,生成式AI,技術合作,市場商機,IP保護

news.Title: 2 AI Infrastructure Stocks That Could Rise 25% and 80% Despite Overdone Spending Fears
news.Content: Two of the hottest stocks in the market right now are Advanced Micro Devices (NASDAQ: AMD) and Micron (NASDAQ: MU) . And according to some Wall Street analysts, these stocks still have more room to run. Baird analyst Tristan Gerra has a street-high $625 price target on AMD, up from $300 ahead of the company's early May earnings report, representing about 25% from here (as of ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) has a street-high price target of $625 from Baird, representing potential 25% upside, driven by inference and agentic AI opportunities.
• Micron (MU) has a price target of $1,625 from UBS, indicating approximately 80% upside, fueled by the memory supercycle and HBM demand.
• AMD is well-positioned for growth in inference and agentic AI markets, leveraging its chiplet design and large GPU deals, and sees opportunity in the data center CPU market.
• Micron benefits from a tight DRAM market due to surging HBM demand, leading to sales growth and margin expansion, with ASML machine bottlenecks constraining supply.
news.Keywords: AI inference, agentic AI, HBM, DRAM market, price targets

AvePoint is broadening beyond Microsoft into a multi-cloud data management and AI security platform, with customer conversations now spanning Google, Salesforce, AWS, Azure and other environments. Executives said the company’s value is strongest in mixed-license, multi-platform deployments where AI ...
news.AI_Summary:

• The company's AgentPulse product is a significant part of its pipeline and may become a standalone offering, boosting its multi-cloud data management and AI security platform.

news.Title: Ford Stock Is Moving Like Tesla Now. Its Results Can’t Justify the Premium.
news.Content: Ford (F) stock has traded in a tight price channel over the last five years. It has tended to bottom near $10, while witnessing selling pressure above $15. However, the stock has rallied over the last month and is now trading at the highest price level in nearly four years. The rally is a welcome break for investors. The only succor has been the generous dividends, which the company has topped up with special dividends in the previous three years to meet its ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Ford's stock price action, particularly its recent rally, is being compared to Tesla's due to its newly launched energy storage business.
• Ford's energy storage business is seen as an 'AI play' with potential for strong growth driven by data center power demand and the need for energy storage systems.
• Ford plans annual capacity of 20 GWh for its energy storage systems starting next year, which is less than half of Tesla's current annual deployment.
• Despite the rally and optimism around the energy storage business, some analysts like Barclays believe Ford's recent gains have been 'overdone', and Morgan Stanley has a $14 target price.
news.Keywords: energy storage, AI play, market comparison, legacy automaker, valuation concerns

news.Title: Australian watchdog sues Amazon unit over children's backpack safety
news.Content: May 29 (Reuters) - Australia's competition watchdog said on Friday that it has initiated legal action against Amazon's local unit, alleging breaches ‌of product safety labelling laws related to children's backpacks. According to ‌the ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Australia's competition watchdog is suing Amazon's local unit for alleged breaches of product safety labelling laws concerning children's backpacks with button batteries.
• The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (ACCC) alleges that 'Unicorn Toddler Backpacks' were supplied without mandatory safety warning labels for button batteries.
• 41 backpacks were sold to consumers, and 267 units were held in Amazon's fulfillment centers between June 22 and November 1, 2022.
• The ACCC is seeking penalties, declarations, and costs, marking its first Federal Court case against an online marketplace for mandatory product safety law breaches.
news.Keywords: product safety, regulatory action, consumer law, button batteries, compliance breach

news.Title: Anthropic Just Delivered Spectacular News For Amazon and Alphabet
news.Content: It isn't quite David defeating Goliath, but Anthropic just did something few would have thought possible a few years ago. The company is now the most valuable AI start-up in the world, surpassing OpenAI with its latest funding round. The owner of the Claude chatbot just closed its Series H round, valuing the company at $965 billion. Anthropic raised $65 billion from investors, including ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Anthropic, an AI startup, has raised $65 billion in its latest funding round, valuing the company at $965 billion, surpassing OpenAI.
• Amazon (AMZN) and Alphabet (GOOGL) are significant investors in Anthropic, with Amazon investing $13 billion (with a further $20 billion pledged) and Alphabet investing up to $40 billion (on top of $13 billion already invested).
• Anthropic's revenue run rate reached $47 billion, and its new Claude Opus 4.8 model outperforms other publicly available AI platforms in benchmarks.
• The funding will be used to expand compute capacity, including significant cloud infrastructure spending with Amazon Web Services (AWS) and Google Cloud.
• Anthropic has pledged to spend $100 billion on AWS and $200 billion on Google Cloud over the next five to ten years.
news.Keywords: AI startup, venture capital, cloud infrastructure, strategic investment, valuation

news.Title: Intel Has Tripled in 2026. Here's 1 Reason It Could Keep Going.
news.Content: A year ago, Intel (NASDAQ: INTC) looked like a company investors had given up on. Its stock briefly traded below $18, its foundry business was bleeding cash, and rivals had lapped it in the race to power artificial intelligence (AI) . So far in 2026, though, the chipmaker has staged one of the more improbable comebacks in big tech, with shares up about 225% as of this writing. Plenty of headlines have fueled the move. There's a reported preliminary agreement to ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Intel (INTC) shares have risen approximately 225% year-to-date, driven by a potential agreement to make chips for Apple and significant cloud customer deals.
• A key catalyst is the increasing importance of CPUs in the AI era, particularly for inference and agentic AI workloads, playing to Intel's core strength.
• Intel's Data Center and AI division revenue grew 22% year-over-year to $5.1 billion, with operating margins more than doubling to ~31%.
• The company's AI-related businesses constitute about 60% of revenue and grew 40% year-over-year last quarter.
• Challenges remain, including a GAAP net loss of $3.7 billion last quarter and a $2.4 billion loss in its foundry unit, with the stock trading at a high valuation.
news.Keywords: CPU, AI workloads, data center, foundry business, valuation

news.Title: Nvidia Already is the GPU Giant. Now It's Aiming to Dominate in a New $200 Billion Market.
news.Content: Nvidia (NASDAQ: NVDA) has built much of its success thanks to one key product: the graphics processing unit (GPU). This is the chip driving key AI tasks such as the training and inference of large language models, and while other companies sell GPUs and similar chips, Nvidia's have consistently been the fastest and most efficient. As a result, customers have rushed to get in on the company's GPUs and latest updates -- and this ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Nvidia (NVDA) is targeting a new $200 billion market with its upcoming Vera Rubin platform, which includes a stand-alone CPU.
• The company expects $20 billion in stand-alone CPU sales this year, aiming to become the leading CPU supplier.
• Nvidia's GPUs are crucial for AI training and inference, driving significant earnings growth; Q1 revenue climbed 85% to $81 billion, and net income advanced 211% to $58 billion.
• The CPU's growing importance in agentic AI, which involves AI taking actions to solve problems, presents a new growth opportunity.
• Dominating both GPU and CPU markets could significantly boost Nvidia's future revenue and expand its market opportunities.
news.Keywords: CPU, GPU, AI market, revenue growth, new market

news.Title: Micron Surges 850% As It Crosses $1 Trillion Valuation
news.Content: This article first appeared on GuruFocus . Micron Technology ( NASDAQ:MU ) has entered rare air. The memory-chip maker's rally has pushed its market value above $1 trillion for the first time, ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Micron Technology (MU) has surpassed a $1 trillion market valuation, marking the fastest such increase on record (48 trading days).
• The surge is driven by strong investor demand for companies involved in AI infrastructure buildout, particularly memory-related semiconductors.
• Micron's shares have gained nearly 850% over the past 12 months, significantly outperforming the Nasdaq 100 and the Philadelphia Semiconductor Index.
• The company's market cap doubled from $500 billion to $1 trillion in just 48 trading days, outpacing historical records set by Samsung and Tesla.
• Analysts expect accelerating earnings growth, with projected adjusted EPS of $105 by fiscal 2027, a more than 1,200% increase from fiscal 2025.
news.Keywords: memory chips, AI infrastructure, market capitalization, valuation, earnings growth

news.Title: Micron Gets Higher Target From DA Davidson
news.Content: This article first appeared on GuruFocus . Micron Technology ( MU , Financials ) received another bullish call as DA Davidson raised its ...
news.AI_Summary:

• The firm suggests a potential shift in how investors view the memory chip market, moving away from a purely commodity-based, cyclical model.
• Increasing reliance on high-bandwidth memory in AI data centers makes Micron's role more critical and potentially less interchangeable.
• Other analysts from UBS, Barclays, and Mizuho have also raised targets, citing strong AI-related memory demand and improved long-term earnings visibility.
• The key question for investors is whether sustained AI demand can maintain strong memory pricing and support higher earnings and cash flow.
news.Keywords: price target, memory market, AI data centers, earnings visibility, valuation

news.Title: Inflation Just Spiked to the Highest Level in Almost 3 Years in the First Report Under New Fed Chair Kevin Warsh. But There Was Some Good News
news.Content: The Federal Reserve’s preferred gauge of inflation came in at the highest level in nearly three years. However, new Fed Chair Kevin Warsh’s first inflation report also contained some good news. The April reading of the personal consumption expenditures price index (PCE) increased 0.4% in April and 3.8% year over year. Will AI create the world's first trillionaire? Our team just released a report on the one little-known company, called an "Indispensable Monopoly" providing the critical technology Nvidia and Intel both need. news.AI_Summary:
• The Federal Reserve's preferred inflation gauge, the PCE price index, rose 0.4% in April and 3.8% year-over-year, reaching a nearly three-year high.
• Core PCE increased 0.2% monthly and 3.3% annually; however, the monthly gains were slightly below economists' projections.
• Higher energy prices, housing, and utilities contributed to the increase, with housing and utilities up 0.6% month-over-month.
• While inflation remains above the Fed's 2% target, the softer-than-expected monthly figures may offer some relief.
• The report is unlikely to significantly alter the Fed's interest rate trajectory, with markets still expecting rates to remain unchanged in the near term.
news.Keywords: inflation, PCE, Federal Reserve, interest rates, economic indicators

news.Title: Robots, Drones, and Physical AI: How to Invest in LiDAR’s Second Act
news.Content: For years, the LiDAR (Light Detection and Ranging) investment story revolved around one idea: autonomous vehicles (AVs). Capital poured into the space through special purpose acquisition vehicles (SPACs) and early-stage listings, all tied to the long-dated promise of self-driving cars. But the market has shifted – quietly at first, as it often does, and then all at once. Today, LiDAR is evolving into the core perception layer for robotics, drones, and physical AI systems, expanding far beyond automotive and creating a broader, more immediate set of investment opportunities in publicly traded markets. More News from Barchart Dear Intel Stock Fans, ...
news.AI_Summary:
• LiDAR technology is expanding beyond autonomous vehicles (AVs) into robotics, drones, and physical AI systems, creating new investment opportunities.
• Robotics-related LiDAR shipments now exceed automotive ADAS volumes, indicating a shift towards higher-volume, faster adoption curves in automation and logistics.
• Technological advancements include cost compression and performance improvements in solid-state and FMCW LiDAR, enabling smaller, more efficient, and longer-range systems (4D LiDAR).
• Expanding use cases for LiDAR-equipped drones include infrastructure inspection, precision agriculture, industrial applications, and mining, driven by demand from sectors like AI data centers.
news.Keywords: LiDAR, robotics, physical AI, drones, investment theme

news.Title: ARM Stock Is Valued for Eternity, But Silicon Has an Expiration Date
news.Content: Looking at the current valuation of Arm Holdings (ARM), the word “astronomical" comes to mind. Arm's market capitalization currently stands at $319 billion. If we glance into the fundamentals, the scale of investors' optimism becomes even more obvious; Arm's annual revenue is around $4.9 billion, while net profit is roughly $900 million. Yes, gross profit sits at an impressive $4.8 billion, ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Arm Holdings (ARM) currently has an 'astronomical' valuation with a market cap of $319 billion against annual revenue of $4.9 billion and net profit of $900 million, resulting in a P/E ratio of 365.
• The company's business model relies on intellectual property (IP) royalties from Arm-based chip production, giving it a dominant position in the current silicon architecture.
• A significant risk to Arm's valuation is the approaching physical limits of traditional silicon chip technology (Moore's Law), with potential paradigm shifts to new technologies like photonic or quantum processors.
• Arm's current IP is tied to silicon, and there is no guarantee of rights to future replacement technologies, suggesting its 'eternal' valuation may be unsustainable due to technological obsolescence.
news.Keywords: valuation, P/E ratio, intellectual property, technological limits, paradigm shift

news.Title: Rational exuberance: Inside Washington’s $2 billion quantum gamble
news.Content: The U.S. Department of Commerce recently sent shockwaves through the technology sector by signing letters of intent to inject $2 billion into nine quantum computing and hardware companies under the CHIPS and Science Act. The capital allocation includes a massive $1 billion commitment to a newly formed IBM subsidiary, alongside substantial stakes in pure-play firms like GlobalFoundries, Rigetti Computing, and D-Wave Quantum. Yet it was not the dollar amount that triggered a parabolic surge in publicly traded quantum stocks—it was the unprecedented structure of the deal. Rather than issuing traditional research grants, the administration is taking minority equity stakes in ...
news.AI_Summary:
• The U.S. Department of Commerce is injecting $2 billion into nine quantum computing and hardware companies via the CHIPS and Science Act, taking minority equity stakes.
• A significant portion, $1 billion, is allocated to an IBM subsidiary, with other investments in companies like GlobalFoundries, Rigetti Computing, and D-Wave Quantum.
• This 'government-as-shareholder' model represents a policy shift towards direct government ownership in strategically critical sectors.
• Industry practitioners view the federal involvement as a validation of the quantum computing space, citing national security and AI leadership as key drivers.
news.Keywords: quantum computing, government investment, equity stakes, CHIPS Act, strategic sectors

news.Title: Is Nvidia the Next Apple?
news.Content: In the world of technology investing, few companies have commanded the level of cultural and financial dominance that Apple (NASDAQ: AAPL) achieved during the first two decades of the 21st century. Yet once the smartphone market matured and rival products became competitive, Apple's hypergrowth phase gave way to a more measured expansion supported by services, ...
news.AI_Summary:
• The article draws parallels between Nvidia's current growth phase driven by AI and Apple's historical dominance in the smartphone market.
• It suggests Nvidia, like Apple, may be transitioning from hypergrowth to a more mature expansion phase, characterized by valuation compression and share buybacks.
• While Nvidia's data center revenue and margins have exploded due to AI, its stock's valuation expansion has moderated.
• The piece highlights Nvidia's untapped opportunities beyond data centers, including CPUs, networking, autonomous driving, and robotics, suggesting it's not yet at a post-innovation plateau.
• Apple's transition involved shifting focus to services and aggressive share repurchases to drive EPS growth when hardware innovation slowed.
news.Keywords: AI growth, market maturation, valuation compression, share buybacks, diversification

news.Title: Micron Technology Just Proved the AI Memory Boom Is Not Cyclical. Here's Why.
news.Content: Micron Technology 's (NASDAQ: MU) meteoric rise over the last year marks more than just a generic bullish upgrade in the semiconductor industry. news.AI_Summary:
• Micron Technology's (MU) recent performance challenges the historical view of memory chip demand being cyclical, driven by structural changes in the AI market.
• AI infrastructure requires continuous High-Bandwidth Memory (HBM) supply, leading hyperscalers to prioritize securing memory over price, creating a demand floor.
• Micron is utilizing long-term agreements with committed volumes and partial price protection, providing predictable revenue streams and reducing inventory risk.
• These contracts enable more deliberate capital expenditure planning, translating into margin expansion and earnings durability, moving away from cyclical tailwinds.
• An analyst raised Micron's price target to $1,625, indicating a belief that the company is a core pillar of the AI infrastructure era, not just experiencing a cyclical wave.
news.Keywords: structural demand, AI infrastructure, HBM, long-term agreements, earnings durability

• AI發展涵蓋雲端、資料中心、硬體與能源供應，未來電力供應及電網升級將成為AI投資的重要關鍵，建議納入電力基建相關基金。
news.Keywords: AI投資,資本支出,供應鏈受惠,基本面支撐,電力基建

news.Title: Should You Buy the Vanguard S&P 500 ETF With the Stock Market at a Record High? History Offers a Clear Answer.
news.Content: The S&P 500 (SNPINDEX: ^GSPC) is a highly diversified stock market index. It tracks 500 companies across 11 sectors of the economy, so it often provides a broad representation of the performance of the American corporate sector. The S&P 500 recently fell as much as 9% from its peak over concerns about how the conflict between the U.S. and Iran would affect the energy market. The index has since recovered fully, thanks to a ceasefire between ...
news.AI_Summary:
• The S&P 500 (SPX) has recovered from a recent dip and shows a year-to-date gain of 9.8%, driven significantly by the Information Technology sector.
• The Information Technology sector, heavily weighted by companies like Nvidia, Apple, and Microsoft, has seen its performance boosted by explosive demand for AI chips, software, and cloud services.
• Excluding the IT sector, the S&P 500's five-year return drops from 78% to 47%, underscoring AI's importance to the current bull market.
• While history suggests continued upside for the Vanguard S&P 500 ETF (VOO), current valuation (21.8x forward earnings) may lead to below-average returns over the next decade, alongside macroeconomic risks like inflation.
news.Keywords: S&P 500, Information Technology, AI demand, market valuation, macroeconomic risk

news.Title: Jim Cramer has bold new take on Elon Musk's SpaceX IPO
news.Content: There is always one IPO that defines a generation of investors. For people who started buying stocks in the 1990s, it was Microsoft ( MSFT ) and Amazon ( AMZN ). For millennials, it was Facebook and Tesla ( TSLA ). Each one arrived with the same problem, a valuation that looked impossible right up until it didn't. Elon Musk 's SpaceX , which has spent nearly a decade as the most-anticipated private company in the world, is about to test the next generation of buyers. SpaceX filed its IPO prospectus on May 20 and is targeting a June 12 ...
news.AI_Summary:
• SpaceX's upcoming IPO is targeting a valuation range of $1.75 trillion to $2 trillion, potentially making it the largest IPO in history.
• Jim Cramer expressed skepticism about the valuation, citing a revenue multiple of approximately 110 times trailing sales, higher than Tesla's at its IPO.
• SpaceX reported $18.7 billion in 2025 revenue (up 33% YoY) but a net loss of $4.94 billion, largely due to $12.7 billion in data center spending for its xAI division.
• Potential catalysts for the bull case include Starship's payload delivery, a $1.25 billion/month compute capacity lease deal with Anthropic, and the potential acquisition of AI startup Cursor.
• The market is evaluating whether SpaceX will be the next Nvidia or WeWork, with significant risks and potential rewards associated with its ambitious valuation and growth initiatives.
news.Keywords: IPO valuation, revenue growth, net loss, capital expenditure, speculative investment

news.Title: From diapers to drugs: How India's global corporate hubs are putting AI to work
news.Content: By Shivansh Tiwary and Praveen Paramasivam May 28 (Reuters) - Whether they are recruiting the ideal "momfluencer" to sell diapers or trawling through reams of data to fast-track drug launches, global centers of multinational ‌firms in India are increasingly putting AI to work in creative ...
news.AI_Summary:

news.Keywords: artificial intelligence, global capability centers, process automation, innovation, healthcare technology
